clinical_history: What is your diagnosis for this "irregular" rhythm with an underlying periodicity? What other abnormalities are noted?
diagnosis: Sinus rhythm is present with very frequent premature atrial complexes (PACs), creating a trigeminal pattern. Left atrial abnormality (LAA) and a prolonged PR interval at about 240 ms (first degree AV "block") are also noted, as well as slow R wave progression in V1-V3, a non-diagnostic finding. The precordial QRS voltage is prominent although strict criteria for left ventricular hypertrophy (LVH) are not met. Keep in mind, however, that the sensitivities of most of the ECG criteria for LVH are relatively low. The premature P waves appear as subtle "bumps" on the T waves, with non-compensatory pauses. This PAC induced "group beating" pattern may simulate second degree AV blocks, but with the latter the sinus P waves come "on time." Frequent PACs, especially with left atrial abnormality, may be a forerunner of atrial fibrillation (AF) or clue to paroxysmal AF.
